name: security-review
description: 3-step security-focused workflow recipe to review code changes for authentication, authorization, secrets, unsafe logging, and dependency risks.

steps.name: map-security-scope
steps.description: Inspect the diff and classify touched surfaces: auth, data access, dependencies, configuration, logging, file IO, network calls, or public API behavior. Produce a concise risk map with changed files and likely threat categories.
steps.producePrompt:
Inspect the git diff of the current changes:

```diff
{{GIT_DIFF}}
```

Classify the touched surfaces in the code changes, including: authentication, authorization/data access, dependencies, configuration, logging, file IO, network calls, or public API behavior. Produce a concise security risk map identifying changed files and their likely threat categories.
steps.rubric:
Score the security risk map report 0-10.

Rubric (10 pts):
  C1 [4] Correctly identifies security-relevant files and changed trust boundaries.
  C2 [3] Classifies touched surfaces into appropriate categories (e.g. auth, data access, logging, config).
  C3 [3] Flags likely threat categories for each modified file.

Output ONLY a single-line JSON object, no prose, no fences:
{"score": <0-10>, "passed": ["C1", "C2", "C3"], "failed": [], "notes": "<one short sentence>"}

steps.name: audit-threats
steps.description: Review the changed code for access-control gaps, injection risks, insecure defaults, unsafe logging, missing validation, and dependency misuse. Emit findings with file references, severity, exploit preconditions, and suggested fixes.
steps.producePrompt:
Perform a security audit on the changed code from the git diff:

```diff
{{GIT_DIFF}}
```

Inspect the code for access-control gaps, injection risks (SQL, command, HTML, etc.), insecure defaults, unsafe logging of sensitive values, missing input validation, and dependency misuse. Output a detailed security audit findings report. For each finding, provide file references, severity (e.g. high, medium, low), exploit preconditions, and suggested fixes.
steps.rubric:
Score the security audit findings report 0-10.

Rubric (10 pts):
  C1 [4] Flags high-risk areas (like unsafe logging, access control gaps) without overstating unproven vulnerabilities.
  C2 [3] Provides exact file references and classifies findings by severity.
  C3 [3] Suggests actionable fixes and outlines exploit preconditions.

Output ONLY a single-line JSON object, no prose, no fences:
{"score": <0-10>, "passed": ["C1", "C2", "C3"], "failed": [], "notes": "<one short sentence>"}

steps.name: generate-security-verdict
steps.description: Aggregate findings into PASS, PASS WITH FOLLOW-UP, or BLOCK. Separate mandatory fixes from hardening recommendations. Include a maintainer checklist for manual verification.
steps.producePrompt:
Aggregate the security findings from previous steps and generate a final security verdict report. The report must contain:
1. A clear security verdict section mapping to PASS, PASS WITH FOLLOW-UP, or BLOCK.
2. A clear separation of mandatory fixes (for BLOCK or PASS WITH FOLLOW-UP) from hardening recommendations.
3. A maintainer checklist for manual verification.
Ensure all examples and artifacts are public-safe and do not expose real incidents or sensitive details.
steps.rubric:
Score the final security verdict report 0-10.

Rubric (10 pts):
  C1 [4] Emits a clear security verdict mapping to PASS, PASS WITH FOLLOW-UP, or BLOCK.
  C2 [3] Distinguishes exploitable, blocking bugs from hardening suggestions, separating mandatory fixes from recommendations.
  C3 [3] Includes a maintainer checklist for manual verification and keeps all examples public-safe.

Output ONLY a single-line JSON object, no prose, no fences:
{"score": <0-10>, "passed": ["C1", "C2", "C3"], "failed": [], "notes": "<one short sentence>"}
